feature_name: v1.19.0 "UI polish" -- a 6-FR small-feature/polish branch (grew from 4 to 6 FRs during Design): one-off download modal select-sizing fix, subscriptions page box-size + layout regression-lock, a new watch-page download-to-device button, a small Setup UI gap-close on the already-shipped synthetic Downloads folder rename/reorder, a v1.18-regression thumbnail-preserve fix (FR-5), and mobile search sizing (FR-6)
branch: feature/v1.19-ui-polish (off main, which is at v1.18.0 -- confirmed via package.json)
base: main (v1.18.0). Prior shipped records live in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (most recently v1.18-ios-playability).
process_plan: AUTONOMOUS OVERNIGHT ROUND. All product forks pre-resolved with stated defaults in the exec plan's Decision log -- nothing left open/blocking for Design or Implementation. FR-1 (one-off modal select-height CSS root cause) and FR-2 (subscriptions box size + a regression-lock on an already-fixed layout bug) are LOW-RISK, CSS/layout-scoped, no security-guard surface -- LIGHTER single-QA no-regression tier, with Dean's on-device iPhone + desktop pass as the documented arbiter for 'feels smooth'/'better proportions' (no headless-browser/E2E infra exists per docs/RELIABILITY.md). FR-3 (new watch-page download-to-device button, reusing the existing id-based /video/:id file-serving route) and FR-4 (a small client-only gap-close on the already-shipped synthetic-folder rename/reorder feature) are SECURITY/DATA-SAFETY-SENSITIVE TOUCHPOINTS -- FR-3 touches the file-serving route (must stay strictly id-based, never accept a client-supplied path, safe Content-Disposition header encoding) and FR-4 touches the folders/config model (the db.folders-vs-folderSettings invariant that a prior data-loss-class bug required fixing) -- PM recommends the TWO-REVIEWER GATE (QA + adversarial /code-review) for both, confirmed at task-breakdown time. IMPORTANT GROUNDING FINDINGS from Discovery (see exec plan Decision log for full detail): FR-2b (the roadmap's 'vertical text' subscriptions bug) and FR-4 (synthetic folder rename/reorder) are BOTH ALREADY FULLY SHIPPED AND TESTED in prior rounds (v1.13.0) -- confirmed by reading the actual code (lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js's dedicated .sub-row layout; server.js's GET/POST /api/config synthetic-folder splice+order+rename logic; public/js/setup.js's already-generic renderFolders(); test/integration/ytdlp-synthetic-folder.test.js's full passing AC42/44/45/46/47/8/9/10/10b/11/12 suite) -- NOT assumed from ROADMAP.md's stale wording. This round's actual new work for those two FRs is narrower than the roadmap implies: FR-2b ships a regression-lock (not a fresh fix); FR-4 ships one small client-only gap-close (disabling the synthetic row's no-op remove button) on top of already-complete server-side behavior. ROADMAP.md itself is stale on both entries -- flagged for EM/Dean housekeeping, not this round's own deliverable.
hard_constraints: NO REGRESSIONS. FR-1/FR-2's CSS changes must not alter the one-off modal's function (URL input, three selects + filetype repopulation on format change, Download button, the v1.17.0 FR-6 backdrop-dismiss full-teardown fix) or the subscriptions page's already-fixed row layout (v1.13.0 AC1 -- .sub-row/.sub-row-info/.sub-row-name/.sub-row-url word-boundary wrapping, never one-character-per-line).

FR-3: the download-intent addition to (or sibling of) GET /video/:id must stay strictly id -> db.metadata[id].filePath -- never accept or interpolate a client-supplied path/filename into any filesystem operation. Must always resolve the ORIGINAL file (item.filePath), bypassing the needsTranscode/transcoded-MP4/live-transcode branch entirely, even when a cached transcode exists. The Content-Disposition filename must be safely encoded (RFC 5987 / filename*=UTF-8'' for non-ASCII titles; no CR/LF/quote header-injection vector from a title string). Must not change /video/:id's existing playback behavior (Range support, 404s, the needsTranscode/?live=1/lazy-transcode branches) for any request that isn't the new download intent.

FR-4: the synthetic Downloads folder must NEVER be written into db.folders -- only into folderSettings[dir] (name + order). This is a repeat of the exact invariant a prior v1.12.0-era data-loss-class bug required fixing. This round's only new work here (disabling the synthetic row's no-op remove button) is client-only in public/js/setup.js and must not touch server.js's GET/POST /api/config synthetic-root handling at all. The existing test/integration/ytdlp-synthetic-folder.test.js suite (AC42/44/45/46/47/8/9/10/10b/11/12) must pass UNMODIFIED as the regression bar.

follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes; textContent over innerHTML for any NEW dynamic strings (FR-4's remove-button tooltip text especially); node:test for new/regression tests; lint 0 warnings; NO new runtime dependencies.

Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties / --vars). Any new/changed CSS (FR-1's select-height fix, FR-2a's list-box sizing, FR-3's Download button, FR-4's disabled-remove-button styling) must use existing theme tokens; no hardcoded colors that break the 2005/2009/2014/2021 eras or light/dark modes.

No headless-browser/E2E test infrastructure exists (per docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- this bounds what FR-1 (mobile select rendering) and FR-2 (row layout/list-box proportions) can claim as [UNIT]/[INTEGRATION]; Dean's on-device iPhone + desktop pass is the documented arbiter for both, not a rigor gap. FR-3 and FR-4 are substantially more code/test-verifiable (an HTTP route's response headers/body; a JSON config round-trip; existing regression suites) with a lighter on-device/visual check layered on top.

FR-2a's list-box size increase must be SCOPED to #sub-list-container specifically (a dedicated rule/modifier), not a blanket change to the shared .folder-list-builder class also used by the Setup folder-management builder (#folders-builder-list) and the one-shot job list (#oneshot-list-container) -- unless Design explicitly documents and justifies a deliberate shared change.
No card-level download affordance (public/js/main.js) is in scope this round for FR-3 -- watch-page only, per the resolved scope fork. A card-level affordance is a deferred fast-follow.

product_forks_for_dean.fr1_select_height: RESOLVED, non-blocking. Target: match .setup-select's existing normal, single-line, content-driven height (~36-40px, padding 10px 12px) -- not the current ~150px+ flex-grown height -- while keeping the existing 44px comfortable-tap-target floor. Root cause confirmed by reading style.css: .oneoff-modal-row select's base flex: 1 1 150px (a width hint for the desktop row) is never reset by the max-width:768px block that flips the row to flex-direction:column, so the 150px flex-basis is reinterpreted as a minimum HEIGHT and flex-grow:1 stretches each select to fill the modal's vertical space. Exact CSS mechanism left to Design.
product_forks_for_dean.fr2a_listbox_scope: RESOLVED, non-blocking. Scope the size increase to #sub-list-container specifically (a dedicated rule/modifier), not the shared .folder-list-builder class. Exact new height/padding values left to Design.
product_forks_for_dean.fr2b_already_fixed: RESOLVED, not an open fork -- confirmed already fixed in v1.13.0 (AC1) by reading lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js's dedicated .sub-row layout and the completed v1.13-polish exec plan. This round ships a regression-lock, not a fresh fix. ROADMAP.md is stale on this entry.
product_forks_for_dean.fr3_mechanism: RESOLVED (default), non-blocking. Extend the existing GET /video/:id route with an explicit download-intent parameter that always serves the original file with Content-Disposition: attachment and a clean filename; a sibling dedicated route sharing the same id lookup is an acceptable Design-stage alternative. Must bypass the needsTranscode branch entirely (confirmed by reading server.js that naive reuse would sometimes serve a transcode instead of the original).
product_forks_for_dean.fr3_scope: RESOLVED, not an open fork -- watch-page only this round per the assignment's own stated default; a card-level affordance is a deferred fast-follow.
product_forks_for_dean.fr4_default_name: RESOLVED, not an open fork -- already 'Downloads' (shipped, unchanged, confirmed in server.js).
product_forks_for_dean.fr4_remove_button_gap: RESOLVED (default), non-blocking, a NEW fork surfaced during Discovery (not pre-resolved by the assignment). The synthetic row's remove ("x") button is a silent no-op (self-heals on the next GET) with no UI signal. Recommend disabling (not hiding) it with an explanatory tooltip -- a small, additive, client-only change that does not touch the db.folders-exclusion invariant.

FR-1.title: One-off download modal polish (mobile oversized selects)
FR-1.review_weight: LIGHTER single-QA no-regression -- CSS/layout-scoped, no security-guard surface. Dean on-device (mobile) + desktop pass is the ARBITER for 'feels smooth/native'.
FR-1.current_behavior_confirmed: style.css's .oneoff-modal-row select base rule (~1910-1920) sets flex: 1 1 150px (a width hint under the default flex-direction:row layout). The max-width:768px mobile block (~2429-2436) flips .oneoff-modal-row to flex-direction:column and sets select { width:100% } but never resets the inherited flex shorthand -- under column direction the 150px flex-basis becomes a MINIMUM HEIGHT and the still-active flex-grow:1 lets each select stretch to fill the modal's vertical space, producing the ~1/4-screen-tall selects in Dean's screenshot. buildOneOffModal (public/js/common.js ~933-1022) itself is unaffected -- CSS-only bug.
FR-1.arbiter: Dean on-device (mobile Safari primary) + desktop for 'feels smooth/native'; the CSS root-cause fix itself is code-verifiable by inspection

FR-2.title: Subscriptions page UI (small box + janky-layout regression-lock)
FR-2.review_weight: LIGHTER single-QA no-regression -- CSS/layout-scoped, no security-guard surface. Dean on-device + desktop pass is the ARBITER for 'better proportions'.
FR-2.current_behavior_confirmed: FR-2a (box too small, GENUINE gap): #sub-list-container uses the shared .folder-list-builder class (style.css ~1545-1553: padding 12px, max-height 240px), also used by #folders-builder-list and #oneshot-list-container -- enlarging it needs to be scoped to the subscriptions list specifically. FR-2b (vertical-text bug, ALREADY FIXED): lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js's createSubscriptionRow/createOneShotRow (lines 261/474) already apply a dedicated .sub-row/.sub-row-info/.sub-row-name/.sub-row-url layout (style.css ~1639-1703) with min-width:240px guard + overflow-wrap/word-break:break-word (never break-all) -- confirmed shipped as v1.13.0's AC1 (docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-06-v1.13-polish.md) and covered by existing tests. ROADMAP.md's 'UI polish' section is stale on this entry. Add/edit/one-shot controls: inspection found no egregious breakage (existing mobile row-gap fix at style.css ~2340-2347) -- light on-device check only.
files: test/unit and test/integration covering the existing .sub-row fix (regression-lock only, no rewrite expected)
FR-2.arbiter: Dean on-device (mobile) + desktop for FR-2a's 'better proportions'; FR-2b is a regression-confirmation, not a fresh bug hunt

FR-3.title: Download-to-device button (new) -- watch page
FR-3.review_weight: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (QA + adversarial /code-review) -- security-sensitive touchpoint: the file-serving route. Must prove the download intent stays strictly id-based/confined and introduces no header-injection vector.
FR-3.current_behavior_confirmed: GET /video/:id (server.js ~2286-2361) looks up db.metadata[id] and serves item.filePath UNLESS item.needsTranscode is true, in which case it serves the cached transcoded MP4 (transcodedPath(item.id)) or a live-transcode stream via ?live=1 -- so naively reusing this route for 'download' would sometimes hand back a transcode, not the original source Dean explicitly wants. No route in server.js sets Content-Disposition today -- genuinely new server-side work. GET /api/videos/:id already spreads the full metadata item (title/ext/filePath) to the client, and public/js/watch.js (~233, ~648) already renders mediaData.filePath (the raw absolute path) directly in the existing UI (file-type text + delete-confirmation modal) -- so FR-3 introduces NO new path-disclosure surface; the requirement is that the actual file-bytes download stays keyed by id, never a client-supplied path.
files: server.js (GET /video/:id or a sibling download route sharing the same id lookup)
public/watch.html (.watch-actions -- new Download button using the existing .icon-download glyph from v1.17.0 FR-7)
FR-3.arbiter: Dean on-device (iPhone/iOS Safari) + desktop (Chrome/Firefox) for the actual save/share-sheet UX; the route's behavior (original-not-transcode, header safety, 404s) is fully code/test-verifiable

FR-4.title: Synthetic Downloads folder: renamable + reorderable in Setup -- ALREADY SHIPPED, one small gap-close
FR-4.review_weight: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (QA + adversarial /code-review) -- security/data-safety-sensitive touchpoint: the db.folders-vs-folderSettings invariant (a prior data-loss-class bug). Gate is to RE-PROVE the invariant holds after the small client-only change, not because new risk is introduced.
FR-4.current_behavior_confirmed: ALREADY FULLY IMPLEMENTED AND TESTED, confirmed by reading server.js's GET/POST /api/config (~1596-1788, 'FR-G part 2'/'Item 3 v1.13.0 order persistence' -- synthetic root spliced into the GET response at a persisted folderSettings[root].order, defaulting name to 'Downloads'; POST accepts a rename+reorder into folderSettings ONLY, explicitly excluded from db.folders even when a client round-trips GET's merged response straight back) and public/js/setup.js's renderFolders() (lines 38-96, already generic -- every row incl. the synthetic one gets the same rename input/hide checkboxes/reorder buttons/drag-and-drop via common.js's shared moveArrayItem/computeDropIndex helpers, 'zero client change' per the v1.13.0 design note, confirmed still true). test/integration/ytdlp-synthetic-folder.test.js already covers AC42/44/45/46/47/8/9/10/10b/11/12 end-to-end and passes today. ROADMAP.md's 'UI polish' section is stale on this entry. ONE genuine gap found: the synthetic row's remove (x) button is a silent no-op (self-heals on the next GET) with no UI signal -- resolved default: disable it with an explanatory tooltip.
files: public/js/setup.js (renderFolders() -- disable the remove button on the synthetic row + tooltip)
public/css/style.css (disabled-button styling, era tokens, if needed)
test/unit (a pure is-synthetic-row decision helper if cleanly extractable)
FR-4.arbiter: code/test-verifiable (the full existing AC42-AC12 suite is the regression bar); light on-device/desktop confirmation of the disabled-remove-button UX

pe_design_items: Route to Design after Discovery. Items needing PE design: FR-1 (the exact CSS mechanism to reset the mobile select's flex-basis/flex-grow without breaking the 44px tap-target floor), FR-2 (the exact #sub-list-container-scoped sizing values, and confirming no shared-class blast radius onto the Setup folder builder / one-shot list), FR-3 (the exact download-intent mechanism -- a query param on GET /video/:id vs. a sibling route -- plus the Content-Disposition filename-encoding approach for non-ASCII titles and the header-injection-safety proof). FR-4 does NOT need PE design (a confirmed-already-shipped feature plus one small, obvious client-only UI change -- disabling a button) but still rides through Design for one cohesive plan and because it shares the two-reviewer gate tier with FR-3.
cross_cutting: FR-1 and FR-2 are independent, CSS/layout-scoped, lower-risk items with no shared files (style.css touches are in different rule blocks) -- LIGHTER single-QA no-regression tier for both, Dean's on-device pass is the documented arbiter. FR-3 (server.js + watch.html/watch.js) and FR-4 (setup.js + style.css) are independent of each other and of FR-1/FR-2 (no shared files) -- both get the TWO-REVIEWER GATE as security/data-safety-sensitive touchpoints (file-serving route confinement for FR-3; the db.folders-vs-folderSettings invariant for FR-4), though neither is loosening an existing guard -- the gate is to prove the new/touched code stays within the existing safe boundaries. No new deps; era-theme CSS vars for all new/changed styling; textContent-not-innerHTML for new dynamic strings (FR-4's tooltip text especially); node:test for new/regression tests; lint 0. No headless-browser/E2E infra exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- FR-1 and FR-2 lean on Dean's on-device + desktop pass as the documented arbiter, not a testing gap; FR-3 and FR-4 ship substantially code/test-verifiable ACs (HTTP route behavior; the existing synthetic-folder regression suite) per docs/CONTRIBUTING.md's 'every new feature or bugfix ships with tests' standard. IMPORTANT: ROADMAP.md's FR-2b and FR-4 entries are stale (both already shipped in v1.13.0) -- flagged to the EM/Dean for a housekeeping update, not this exec plan's own deliverable.
acceptance_verdict: ACCEPT-WITH-NOTES: 53/53 tracked acceptance criteria met across FR-1..FR-6 + Cross-cutting (docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-08-v1.19-ui-polish.md, Acceptance section). All code/test-verifiable criteria PASS on direct code + test inspection (build 1249/1249 green on Node 22, lint clean, QA APPROVED, adversarial pass clean with 3 items proactively remediated -- exec->execFile ffmpeg hardening, RFC-5987 '%27' apostrophe encoding, disabled-button cursor -- all 3 independently confirmed present in code). Every [MANUAL] criterion (FR-1/FR-2/FR-3/FR-5/FR-6 visual polish, iPhone save UX, icon-restore) is structurally satisfied by the code and awaits Dean's documented on-device pass -- the plan's own designed arbiter (no headless-browser/E2E infra per docs/RELIABILITY.md), not a gap. Two non-blocking notes: (1) Dean's on-device pass still outstanding before full end-to-end verification; (2) ROADMAP.md's FR-2b/FR-4 stale entries remain unedited -- explicitly out of this exec plan's scope per Discovery, flagged as a separate housekeeping item. Recommend advancing to Done once Dean's on-device pass confirms the MANUAL items. NOTE: this feature-state.json's stage/scope_items were stale relative to the exec plan (which already had a completed Design section for FR-1..FR-6) at the start of this Acceptance pass -- reviewed directly against the exec plan and code per the EM's explicit assignment; flagged for EM housekeeping.

history.note: PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery for v1.19.0 'UI polish', a 4-FR small-feature/polish branch (feature/v1.19-ui-polish, off main at v1.18.0, confirmed via package.json) run as an autonomous overnight round. Read docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md; ROADMAP.md's 'UI polish' section (source for all 4 items); the completed v1.16/v1.17/v1.18 exec plans for architectural context and precedent on tagging/forks/review-tier conventions. Grounded every AC directly against the current code, not assumptions from ROADMAP.md's wording: FR-1's oversized-select root cause traced to a specific style.css interaction (.oneoff-modal-row select's flex:1 1 150px width-hint reinterpreted as a min-height under the mobile flex-direction:column override, which never resets it); FR-2a's #sub-list-container confirmed genuinely small (shared .folder-list-builder class, 240px/12px); FR-2b's 'vertical text' bug confirmed ALREADY FIXED in v1.13.0 (read lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js's dedicated .sub-row layout directly, cross-checked against docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-06-v1.13-polish.md's shipped AC1) -- ROADMAP.md is stale here; FR-3's naive-reuse pitfall found by reading server.js's GET /video/:id directly (it serves the TRANSCODED file when needsTranscode is true, so a download intent must explicitly bypass that branch to serve the original) and confirmed no Content-Disposition exists anywhere in server.js today (genuinely new work), while also confirming the raw filePath is ALREADY displayed client-side in watch.js today (no new disclosure surface); FR-4 confirmed ALREADY FULLY IMPLEMENTED AND TESTED by reading server.js's GET/POST /api/config synthetic-folder splice/rename/order logic, public/js/setup.js's already-generic renderFolders(), and test/integration/ytdlp-synthetic-folder.test.js's full passing AC42-AC12 suite -- ROADMAP.md is stale here too; the only genuine new gap is the synthetic row's no-op remove button, resolved with a disable-plus-tooltip default. Confirmed no conflict with any other active exec plan (none existed at Discovery start), no contradiction with docs/ARCHITECTURE.md's documented key decisions (none of which this round's FRs touch at the mechanism level), and no open docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md item this round creates or addresses. Authored docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-08-v1.19-ui-polish.md: Goal/Scope (FR-1..FR-4, each with a grounded current-behavior-confirmed subsection)/Out-of-scope/Constraints/8-fork Decision-log-backed resolutions (all resolved with stated defaults per the autonomous-round instruction, zero left open/blocking)/grouped-tagged Acceptance criteria (FR-1 x5, FR-2 x6, FR-3 x8, FR-4 x6, Cross-cutting x7 = 32 criteria total) + a Decision log recording the root-cause findings and the two ALREADY-SHIPPED discoveries (FR-2b, FR-4) in detail. Flagged FR-3 (file-serving route) and FR-4 (folders/config db.folders-vs-folderSettings invariant) for the two-reviewer gate; FR-1/FR-2 recommended for a lighter single-QA no-regression tier, with Dean's on-device iPhone + desktop pass as the documented, non-negotiable arbiter for FR-1's 'feels smooth/native' and FR-2's 'better proportions', per docs/RELIABILITY.md's lack of headless-browser/E2E infrastructure. Set artifacts.exec_plan/requirements/design all to the new exec plan path; stage remains 'discovery' (did not self-advance). Did not write application/test code and did not touch git (coordinator-owned). Reporting back to EM for Design routing (/prep-pe-design); also flagged ROADMAP.md's two stale entries (FR-2b, FR-4) for an EM/Dean housekeeping update outside this exec plan's own deliverable.
